metadata.name: lhs_144_6param
metadata.description:
Latin Hypercube Sampling sweep at 144-block retarget expanding to 6 active parameters.

PURPOSE: The existing lhs_2016_full_parameter sweep varied 4 parameters
(economic_split, pool_committed_split, pool_ideology_strength, pool_max_loss_pct)
but held pool_profitability_threshold and solo_miner_hashrate fixed at assumed values.
Neither of these has ever been varied in any prior sweep, despite both directly
affecting the pool cascade trigger mechanism:
  - pool_profitability_threshold: minimum profit advantage required for pools to switch
    forks; directly controls when the cascade fires; fixed at 0.16 throughout Phase 1
  - solo_miner_hashrate: structural v27 hashrate advantage baked into every scenario;
    may shift the Foundry flip-point and ESP; fixed at 0.085 throughout Phase 1

This sweep tests whether these assumptions are load-bearing by including both
parameters in an unbiased LHS alongside the 4 established active parameters.

DESIGN:
- 130 scenarios via Latin Hypercube Sampling (26 per namespace, 5 namespaces)
- 6 parameters varied across plausible ranges
- 144-block retarget for comparison with targeted Phase 1 sweeps
- Duration 13000s

RUNTIME: 130 scenarios x ~35 min = ~76 hours sequential
         With 5 namespaces: ~15.2 hours

GENERATES: Unbiased 144-block feature importance across 6D parameter space.
           If pool_profitability_threshold and solo_miner_hashrate show near-zero
           separation, they are confirmed non-causal and can be fixed in Phase 3.
           If they show non-trivial separation, the Phase 3 LHS must be 6D.
